        dtype=zeros_dtype,
        device=device,
    )

    iweights_triton = awq_dequantize_triton(qweight, scales, zeros)

    assert not torch.any(torch.isinf(iweights_triton)) and not torch.any(
        torch.isnan(iweights_triton)
    )

    iweights_torch = awq_dequantize_torch(qweight, scales, zeros, group_size)

    torch.testing.assert_close(iweights_triton, iweights_torch)

test_dequantize(128, 128, 32)

title: [Feature] Support SIMD/SIMT mix compile for unstructured memory access
body:
## Background

Triton-Ascend currently handles most Triton memory accesses through the SIMD-oriented lowering path. For kernels that contain indirect or semi-structured memory access patterns, this path either falls back to scalarized loop expansion or requires the SIMT template path, which limits the ability to use native HFusion gather/scatter operations.

We need a compile mode that can keep the normal SIMD pipeline for structured computation while routing unstructured load/store operations to a SIMT-capable or HFusion-native path.

## Goal

Add a `compile_mode="simd_simt"` option that enables mixed SIMD/SIMT compilation for unstructured memory access on 910_95-class targets. In this mode, unstructured `tl.load` / `tl.store` patterns should be recognized, represented explicitly in the IR, and lowered to `hfusion.gather_load` / `hfusion.scatter_store`, using a conservative `burstlen=1` when the burst length cannot be statically determined.

## Scope

- Introduce compile-mode based routing for `simd`, `simd_simt`, `simt_template`, and existing compatibility modes.
- Identify unstructured pointer offsets and discrete-mask memory accesses before Linalg lowering.
- Add intermediate `ascend.unstructured_load` / `ascend.unstructured_store` ops to carry base pointer, indices, mask, and unstructured-dim metadata.
- Lower intermediate ops to HFusion gather/scatter in `simd_simt` mode, with fallback to SIMT template calls when needed.
- Keep `force-simt-template` accepted as a deprecated pass-option alias for existing MLIR pipelines.
- Add tests covering indirect load/store, index select/put style semi-structured access, pass option compatibility, and MLIR conversion behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `compile_mode="simd_simt"` is rejected on unsupported A2/A3 targets and accepted on 910_95-class targets.
- Unstructured load/store ops are produced by `TritonToUnstructure` in mix compile modes.
- `TritonToLinalg` lowers static-burst unstructured ops to HFusion gather/scatter in `simd_simt` mode.
- Dynamic burst-length cases do not emit invalid constants; they conservatively use `burstlen=1` on the HFusion gather/scatter path.
- Existing `simd` behavior remains unchanged.
- Existing `force-simt-template` MLIR pass pipelines remain parseable during the transition to `compile-mode`.

title: triton-opt and bishengir-compile command lines printed with TRITON_DEBUG=1 should be copy-pastable without changes
body:
If `TRITON_DEBUG=1` is set, we print `triton-opt` and `bishengir-compile` command lines that greatly aid debugging:

```
TRITON_ALWAYS_COMPILE=1 TRITON_DEBUG=1 TRITON_DUMP_DIR=./dump python3 01-vector-add.py
...
[DEBUG] cmd list: /home/abokhanko/bisheng/triton-ascend/build/lib.linux-x86_64-cpython-311/triton/_C/triton-opt /tmp/tmpeeekbnge/kernel.ttir.mlir '--pass-pipeline=any(auto-blockify{auto-blockify-size=1},triton-to-structured{enable-mask-fallback-conversion=false optimize-dynamic-offset=false},discrete-mask-access-conversion{compile-on-910-95=false enable-sync-block-lock=false force-simt-template=false},triton-to-annotation,triton-to-unstructure{compile-on-910-95=false force-scalarize-mode=false force-simt-template=false},triton-to-hivm,triton-to-hfusion,triton-to-llvm,bubble-up-operation{enable-aggressive-mode=true},triton-to-structured{enable-mask-fallback-conversion=false optimize-dynamic-offset=false},triton-to-linalg{compile-on-910-95=false enable-nd2nz-on-vector=false enable-select-analysis=true global-kernel=false named-ops=true})' --mlir-print-debuginfo -o /tmp/tmpeeekbnge/kernel.ttadapter.mlir
[DEBUG] cmd_list: /home/abokhanko/bisheng/AscendNPU-IR/build/bin/bishengir-compile /tmp/tmpzz5sbupb/kernel.mlir --target=Ascend910B4-1 --enable-auto-multi-buffer=True --enable-auto-bind-sub-block=True --link-aicore-bitcode=/home/abokhanko/bisheng/triton-ascend/build/lib.linux-x86_64-cpython-311/triton/backends/ascend/lib/libdevice.10.bc --enable-hfusion-compile=true --enable-hivm-compile=true --enable-triton-kernel-compile=true --bishengir-print-ir-after=hivm-graph-sync-solver -o /tmp/tmpzz5sbupb/kernel
```

These lines are not copy-pastable without changes, though, for [at least] two reasons:
1) They refer to files in `/tmp/something/` dirs, even when `TRITON_DUMP_DIR` is set.
2) Options might include spaces inside (for example, `--append-bisheng-options=-cce-link-aicore-ll-module file.bc` added at https://github.com/triton-lang/triton-ascend/blob/016bec131df8ec738a4e4f705c6a939c4a6e23bc/third_party/ascend/backend/compiler.py#L814).

This should be fixed and the printed command lines should be copy-pastable without any changes at all.

title: TRITON_DEBUG and @triton.jit(debug=True) can produce conflicting debug states
body:
### Describe the bug

## Description

There is currently a mismatch between the value of the `TRITON_DEBUG` environment variable and the effective compiler debug option.

For example:

```python
import triton

@triton.jit(debug=True)
def kernel(...):
    ...
```

and

```bash
export TRITON_DEBUG=0
```

result in the following state inside `compiler.py`:

```text
TRITON_DEBUG=False
opt.debug=True
```

This creates an inconsistent debug configuration because some parts of the compilation pipeline may rely on `opt.debug`, while others may directly read `TRITON_DEBUG`.

## Expected behavior

There should be a single source of truth for the debug mode.

Possible approaches:

1. Treat `opt.debug` as the authoritative value and avoid reading `TRITON_DEBUG` directly in the backend and C++ passes.
2. Synchronize `TRITON_DEBUG` and `opt.debug` during compilation.
3. At minimum, emit a warning when the two values differ.

## Why this matters

The current behavior can lead to situations where:
* kernel compilation runs in debug mode (`opt.debug=True`),
* backend passes or utilities behave as if debug mode is disabled (`TRITON_DEBUG=False`),

This makes debugging behavior difficult to reason about and may produce inconsistent dumps, logging, or optimization decisions.

### Environment details

title: 950PR上跑fp8算子coredump，报错Assertion `isa<To>(Val) && "cast<Ty>() argument of incompatible type!"' failed
body:
### Describe the bug

算子代码如下：
# Copyright © 2026 Huawei Technologies Co., Ltd.
import math
import torch
import triton
import triton.language as tl

DEVICE = (
    "cuda"
    if torch.cuda.is_available()
    else "npu"
    if torch.npu.is_available()
    else "cpu"
)


@triton.jit
def _per_token_group_quant_fp8(
    x_ptr,
    x_q_ptr,
    x_s_ptr,
    group_size,
    num_groups,
    y_num_columns,
    y_row_stride,
    eps,
    fp8_min: tl.constexpr,
    fp8_max: tl.constexpr,
    use_ue8m0: tl.constexpr,
    BLOCK_SIZE: tl.constexpr,
    N: tl.constexpr,
):
    groups_per_row = y_num_columns // group_size
    local_pid = tl.program_id(0)
    M = tl.num_programs(0)

    # ====================== 循环处理多个 group ======================
    for iN in range(N):
        # 正确计算全局 group ID
        g_id = local_pid + iN * M

        # 计算属于第几行、第几个group
        row = g_id // groups_per_row
        row_g_id = g_id % groups_per_row

        # ====================== 【关键】每次循环都重新计算指针，不使用 += ======================
        x_g_ptr = x_ptr + (row * y_row_stride) + (row_g_id * group_size)
        x_q_g_ptr = x_q_ptr + g_id * group_size
        x_s_g_ptr = x_s_ptr + g_id

        # 加载
        cols = tl.arange(0, BLOCK_SIZE)
        mask = (cols < group_size) & (g_id < num_groups)
        x = tl.load(x_g_ptr + cols, mask=mask, other=0.0)

        # 量化计算
        _absmax = tl.maximum(tl.max(tl.abs(x)), eps)
        scale_raw = _absmax * (1.0 / fp8_max)
        x_s = tl.math.exp2(tl.ceil(tl.log2(scale_raw))) if use_ue8m0 else scale_raw
        x_q = tl.clamp(x / x_s, fp8_min, fp8_max).to(x_q_ptr.dtype.element_ty)

        # 写回（每次都用新指针，不叠加）
        tl.store(x_q_g_ptr + cols, x_q, mask=mask)
        tl.store(x_s_g_ptr, x_s, mask=g_id < num_groups)


@triton.jit
def _per_token_group_quant_fp8_colmajor(
    x_ptr,
    x_q_ptr,
    x_s_ptr,
    group_size,
    num_groups,
    y_num_columns,
    y_row_stride,
    y_s_col_stride,
    eps,
    fp8_min: tl.constexpr,
    fp8_max: tl.constexpr,
    use_ue8m0: tl.constexpr,
    BLOCK_SIZE: tl.constexpr,
    N: tl.constexpr,
):
    groups_per_row = y_num_columns // group_size
    local_pid = tl.program_id(0)
    M = tl.num_programs(0)

    for iN in range(N):
        g_id = local_pid + iN * M
        row = g_id // groups_per_row
        row_g_id = g_id % groups_per_row

        # ====================== 【关键】每次循环重新计算，不 += ======================
        x_g_ptr = x_ptr + row * y_row_stride + row_g_id * group_size
        x_q_g_ptr = x_q_ptr + g_id * group_size

        blocks_per_row = groups_per_row
        scale_col = g_id % blocks_per_row
        scale_row = g_id // blocks_per_row

        # scale 指针正确计算
        scale_offset = scale_col * y_s_col_stride + scale_row
        x_s_g_ptr = x_s_ptr + scale_offset

        cols = tl.arange(0, BLOCK_SIZE)
        mask = (cols < group_size) & (g_id < num_groups)

        x = tl.load(x_g_ptr + cols, mask=mask, other=0.0)
        _absmax = tl.maximum(tl.max(tl.abs(x)), eps)
        scale_raw = _absmax * (1.0 / fp8_max)
        x_s = tl.math.exp2(tl.ceil(tl.log2(scale_raw))) if use_ue8m0 else scale_raw
        x_q = tl.clamp(x / x_s, fp8_min, fp8_max).to(x_q_ptr.dtype.element_ty)

        tl.store(x_q_g_ptr + cols, x_q, mask=mask)
        tl.store(x_s_g_ptr, x_s, mask=g_id < num_groups)


def triton_per_token_group_quant_fp8(
    x: torch.Tensor,
    group_size: int,
    dtype: torch.dtype,
    column_major_scales: bool = False,
    block_size=None,
    eps: float = 1e-10,
    use_ue8m0: bool = False,
) -> tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]:
    assert x.shape[-1] % group_size == 0, (
        f"the last dimension of `x` {x.shape[-1]} must be divisible "
        f"by `group_size` {group_size}"
    )
    assert x.stride(-1) == 1, "`x` must be contiguous on the last dimension"

    x = x.to(DEVICE)
    fp8_min = torch.finfo(dtype).min
    fp8_max = torch.finfo(dtype).max

    # 输出tensor分配
    x_q = torch.empty(x.shape, device=x.device, dtype=dtype)
    if column_major_scales:
        shape = x.shape[:-2] + (x.shape[-1] // group_size, x.shape[-2])
        x_s = torch.empty(shape, device=x.device, dtype=x.dtype).permute(-1, -2)
    else:
        shape = x.shape[:-1] + (x.shape[-1] // group_size,)
        x_s = torch.empty(shape, device=x.device, dtype=x.dtype)

    num_groups = x.numel() // group_size

    # Grid 配置（NPU自动获取VectorCore数量）
    if DEVICE == "cuda":
        block_size = triton.next_power_of_2(group_size)
        N = 1
        grid = (num_groups,)
    else:
        block_size = group_size
        import triton.runtime.driver as driver

        device_idx = torch.npu.current_device()
        core_num = driver.active.utils.get_device_properties(device_idx)[
            "num_vectorcore"
        ]
        grid = (core_num,)
        N = (num_groups + core_num - 1) // core_num

    # 启动kernel
    if column_major_scales:
        _per_token_group_quant_fp8_colmajor[grid](
            x,
            x_q,
            x_s,
            group_size,
            num_groups,
            x.shape[1],
            x.stride(0),
            x_s.stride(1),
            eps,
            fp8_min,
            fp8_max,
            use_ue8m0,
            block_size,
            N,
        )
    else:
        # 【修复】统一使用grid启动，不写死(M,)
        _per_token_group_quant_fp8[grid](
            x,
            x_q,
            x_s,
            group_size,
            num_groups,
            x.shape[1],
            x.stride(0),
            eps,
            fp8_min,
            fp8_max,
            use_ue8m0,
            block_size,
            N,
        )

    return x_q, x_s



测试代码如下：

title: [packaging] triton-ascend conflicts with mainline triton over triton/backends/compiler.py namespace
body:
## Summary

`triton-ascend` 3.2.0/3.2.1 and mainline `triton` (3.3+) cannot coexist in the same site-packages. Both pip packages claim ownership of `triton/backends/compiler.py` and `triton/backends/__init__.py`, and the two forks of `compiler.py` have diverged enough that mainline Triton's `triton/backends/amd/compiler.py` fails to import once `triton-ascend` overwrites the file.

Symptom — `import triton` raises:

```
ImportError: cannot import name 'Language' from 'triton.backends.compiler'
  (/usr/local/python3.11.14/lib/python3.11/site-packages/triton/backends/compiler.py)
  at triton.backends.__init__:_discover_backends
  via triton.backends.amd.compiler line 1:
    from triton.backends.compiler import BaseBackend, GPUTarget, Language
```

This is not specific to the AMD backend — `_discover_backends()` iterates all backends, so any `import triton` (Ascend or otherwise) fails.

## Why this matters

Downstream NPU integrators frequently need both:

- `triton-ascend` for the Triton DSL on Ascend
- `vllm` / `vllm-ascend`, which transitively requires `xgrammar`, which in turn `Requires: triton` (mainline)

The current packaging makes these requirements mutually incompatible by construction, even though the actual code surfaces don't conflict — only the namespace squat does. The user has to apply a fragile post-install fix (`pip uninstall triton && pip install --force-reinstall --no-deps triton-ascend`) after every environment recreation, which makes container builds and CI scripts brittle.

I notice the project README announces a planned Triton 3.5 upgrade for 2026 — that's great, but the packaging conflict is orthogonal to which mainline Triton version you track. Even at Triton 3.5, the two `compiler.py` files will continue to diverge (Huawei-specific symbols like `AttrsDescriptor`, mainline-specific symbols like `Language`/[future]), so the underlying issue persists.

## Environment

- Ascend A3 NPU (dav-c220), CANN 8.5.0
- Container: `quay.io/ascend/verl:verl-8.5.2-a3-ubuntu22.04-py3.11-qwen3-5`
- Python 3.11.14
- `pip show` reports:
  - `triton 3.6.0` (Required-by: xgrammar; xgrammar Required-by: vllm, vllm_ascend)
  - `triton-ascend 3.2.0`

## Reproduce

```bash
# In a fresh Python env on NPU host
pip install vllm-ascend            # transitively installs xgrammar → triton 3.6.0
pip install triton-ascend           # bundled compiler.py overwrites mainline
python -c "import triton; print(triton.__version__)"
# -> ImportError: cannot import name 'Language' from 'triton.backends.compiler'
```

## Root cause (verified)

`pip show -f triton-ascend` reports:

```
triton/backends/__init__.py
triton/backends/compiler.py
triton/backends/ascend/__init__.py
triton/backends/ascend/compiler.py
```

`pip show -f triton` reports:

```
triton/backends/__init__.py
triton/backends/compiler.py
triton/backends/amd/__init__.py
triton/backends/amd/compiler.py
triton/backends/nvidia/__init__.py
triton/backends/nvidia/compiler.py
```

The two `compiler.py` files have completely different exported symbols:

| File source | Exports `class Language` | Exports `AttrsDescriptor` | Size (bytes) |
|---|---|---|---|
| Mainline `triton 3.6.0` (verified via `zipfile` read of the PyPI wheel) | ✅ | ❌ | 2,834 |
| `triton-ascend 3.2.0` (this package's bundled `python/triton/backends/compiler.py`) | ❌ | ✅ | 11,388 |

Whichever package is installed later wins the file. If `triton-ascend` is the later install, mainline triton's `amd/compiler.py` and `nvidia/compiler.py` (still on disk, still discovered by `_discover_backends`) try to import `Language` from a file that no longer exports it → ImportError.

`triton-ascend`'s pypi metadata (`pip show triton-ascend`) currently reports:

```
Provides-Dist: <none>
Provides: <none>
Conflicts: <none>
```

So pip's resolver treats `triton` and `triton-ascend` as independent packages and happily installs both. There's no signal to pip — or to downstream packages like xgrammar — that triton-ascend is a substitute implementation of triton.

## Possible fix directions

(Posted for visibility — happy to author whichever direction the team picks, since I have the empirical setup ready.)

1. **`Provides-Dist: triton (==3.X.Y)` in `setup.py`** — makes pip treat `triton-ascend` as fulfilling a `triton` requirement, so xgrammar/vllm/etc. don't pull in mainline triton when triton-ascend is already installed. Easy; non-invasive; right answer if triton-ascend's DSL surface is a strict-enough match for downstream Triton API consumers (which it appears to be, given Ascend's existing user base).
2. **`Conflicts: triton` (or PEP 508 advisory)** — refuse to install both. Eliminates ambiguous-state failure but forces users to pick at install time.
3. **Namespace packaging** — move triton-ascend's contents under `triton_ascend.backends.ascend` and patch into `triton.backends` at runtime via a Triton entry point hook. Higher engineering cost but no file-level overlap; would survive arbitrary future Triton-mainline `compiler.py` changes.
4. **Install-guide doc + post-install hook** — keep the current packaging but warn at install time and provide `triton-ascend-fix-install` shell helper that removes mainline triton's `backends/amd`/`backends/nvidia` directories if present. Least intrusive change but pushes responsibility to users.

I'd vote for (1) since it requires only a few lines in `setup.py`, but I understand the team may want (3) for the Triton 3.5 upgrade.

## Workaround (verified working today)

```bash
pip uninstall -y triton
pip install --force-reinstall --no-deps triton-ascend
```

After this, `import triton` works, `triton.__version__ == 3.2.0`, and `triton.backends.backends == {'ascend': ...}`. Verified on the affected container running: `mindspeed.megatron_adaptor` import OK, miles NPU end-to-end at H=64 SEQ=2048 real DSv4-Flash shape OK, full forward+backward+Adam step OK. xgrammar warns that mainline triton is missing, but its inference-grammar path is not exercised in NPU training workloads.

## Note on the repo move

I see the project moved from `gitcode.com/Ascend/triton-ascend` to `github.com/triton-lang/triton-ascend` and that gitcode is being archived — I'm filing here at the canonical home. The issue applies to both 3.2.0 and 3.2.1 (just released).

Thanks for taking a look.
